titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Melvin Bernhardt Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke Staff, Annalise Hennessey

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday March 18, 2025 at 00:22

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection includes audiovisual media, certificates, correspondence, financial papers, newspaper clippings, notes, pencil sketches, photographs, playbills, resumes, and scripts for plays directed by Melvin Bernhardt.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection includes audiovisual media, certificates, correspondence, financial papers, newspaper clippings, notes, pencil sketches, photographs, playbills, resumes, and scripts for plays directed by Melvin Bernhardt, including John Guare's Cop-Out , Beth Henley's Crimes of the Heart , Hugh Leonard's Da , and Paul Zindel's The Effect of Gamma Rays on Man-in-the-Moon Marigolds .

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 1-2 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

bioghist.head: Melvin Bernhardt (1931-2015)
bioghist.p: Born Melvin Bernhard in Buffalo, New York, Bernhardt attended the University of Buffalo and Yale University before beginning his career directing regional theater. In 1965, Bernhardt made his directorial debut in New York City with Off-Broadway productions of John Guare's Cop-out and Muzeeka . He also directed The Effect of Gamma Rays on Man-in-the-Moon Marigolds in 1970. In addition to a Tony Award for Hugh Leonard's Da , Bernhardt won the 1978 Drama Desk Award for directing Da . In 1974, Bernhardt began directing American daytime television dramas, including Another World , One Life to Live , and All My Children .

In 2011, Bernhardt married actor Jeff Woodman, known for his roles in television shows like Person of Interest , Law and Order: Special Victims Unit , and Law and Order . Bernhardt died on September 12, 2015 in New York City.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Melvin Bernhardt Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Melvin Bernhardt Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. June 2018 Acquisition, 1955-2015. II. July 2019 Acquisition, 1955-1969, undated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains playbills, notes, correspondence, newspaper clippings, bound scripts, photographs, and other papers related to the career of Melvin Bernhardt.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into four subseries: Production Materials, Bound Scripts, Other Papers, and Audiovisual Material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains playbills, notes, correspondence, and newspaper clippings relating to Bernhardt's work as director of several plays, including The Ballad of the Sad Café , Crimes of the Heart , Da , and The Effects of Gamma Rays on Man-In-The-Moon Marigolds .

title.part: And Miss Reardon Drinks A Little

did.unittitle: And Miss Reardon Drinks A Little materials and correspondence

title.part: The Effect of Gamma Rays on Man-In-The-Moon Marigolds
Is There Life After High School?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains copies of scripts and notes for Jan Buttram's Texas Homos , Roger Hedden's Count , Jerome Kilty's Margaret Sanger/Unfinished Business , Jeffrey Kindly's Is There Life After High School? , Lezley Havard's Hide and Seek , and A.R. Gurney Jr.'s What I Did Last Summer , among others.

did.unittitle: Bernhardt, Melvin. The Piped Piper of Hamelin
Broad, Jay. In Honored Memory of a Quince Tree
Capote, Truman. Other Voices Other Rooms

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Adapted for the stage by Anna Marie Barlow.

scopecontent.p: Adapted for the stage by Edward Albee.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains financial papers, resumes, certificates, photographs, and other personal papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains audiovisual material related to Bernhardt's career as a director.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged under two subheadings: Audiocassettes and Videocassettes. Within each subheading, materials maintain original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains various bound scripts relating to Melvin Bernhardt's work as a director.
